segments.text: However, Job, please hear my speech, and listen to all my words.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: See now, I have opened my mouth. My tongue has spoken in my mouth.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: My words will utter the uprightness of my heart. That which my lips know they will speak sincerely.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If you can, answer me. Set your words in order before me, and st and up.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Behold, I am toward God even as you are. I am also formed out of the clay.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Behold, my terror will not make you afraid, neither will my pressure be heavy on you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: “Surely you have spoken in my hearing, I have heard the voice of your words, saying,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: ‘I am clean, without disobedience. I am innocent, neither is there iniquity in me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Behold, he finds occasions against me. He counts me for his enemy.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He puts my feet in the stocks. He marks all my paths.’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: “Behold, I will answer you. In this you are not just, for God is greater than man.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Why do you strive against him, because he doesn’t give account of any of his matters?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For God speaks once, yes twice, though man pays no attention.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text:
In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on men, 
in slumbering on the bed,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: then he opens the ears of men, and seals their instruction,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: that he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He keeps back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: “He is chastened also with pain on his bed, with continual strife in his bones,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: so that his life abhors bread, and his soul dainty food.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: His flesh is so consumed away that it can’t be seen. His bones that were not seen stick out.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Yes, his soul draws near to the pit, and his life to the destroyers.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
“If there is beside him an angel, an interpreter, one among a thousand, 
to show to man what is right for him,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
then God is gracious to him, and says,‘Deliver him from going down to the pit, 
I have found a ransom.’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: His flesh will be fresher than a child’s. He returns to the days of his youth.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
He prays to God, and he is favorable to him, so that he sees his face with joy. 
He restores to man his righteousness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
He sings before men, and says,‘I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, 
and it didn’t profit me.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He has redeemed my soul from going into the pit. My life will see the light.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: “Behold, God does all these things, twice, yes three times, with a man,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: to bring back his soul from the pit, that he may be enlightened with the light of the living.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Mark well, Job, and listen to me. Hold your peace, and I will speak.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If you have anything to say, answer me. Speak, for I desire to justify you.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If not, listen to me. Hold your peace, and I will teach you wisdom.
segments.speaker: Elihu
